In contrast, alloys with high FCC fractions (e.g., Al16.67-Co16.67-Cr16.67-Fe16.67-Ni33.32) exhibit poor oxidation performance due to limited Al supply for continuous Al₂O₃ formation, leading to Cr₂O₃ spallation (weight change -0.07 mg/cm²) and higher oxidation rates (K_p ~10⁻⁹ g²cm⁻⁴s⁻¹).
